_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, which averages the prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice indexed to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 141, indicating a 41% increase since 2015. This rise is recent and uneven across the components, reflecting varied market dynamics rather than a uniform price increase. The index aggregates globally traded soft commodity prices, providing a snapshot of how these essential breakfast staples have become more expensive over the past several years.
notes.movers: Coffee is the largest contributor, up 99% since 2015, followed by orange juice at 34%, wheat at 19%, and sugar at 13%. These price changes are driven predominantly by weather events and crop diseases, such as frost or drought in arabica coffee regions and citrus greening or hurricanes affecting orange juice production. These are thin markets where supply shocks can cause sharp, localized price moves rather than broad-based inflationary pressures.
notes.why: The headline basket increase is primarily due to vulnerabilities in just a few weather-sensitive crops. Given the nature of soft commodity markets, which are exposed to environmental volatility and disease, elevated prices can persist for extended periods. This underscores the importance of monitoring climatic and biological factors influencing supply.
